summary: Russia launched a massive drone attack on Ukraine overnight, with Ukrainian channels reporting approximately 10 drones heading toward Moscow and Russian authorities claiming 67 drones shot down, while Vnukovo and Domodedovo airports were closed. Ukraine faces continued ballistic missile threats, with alerts for Iskander-M launches from Bryansk Oblast and Tsirkon missiles targeting Kyiv. On the ground, Russian forces continue offensive operations across multiple sectors including Kharkov, Krasny Liman, Konstantinovka, and the Zaporozhye region, while Ukrainian forces struck the Barrikady plant in Volgograd and are conducting FPV drone operations against Russian logistics heading to Crimea and the front line.
key_points: Massive drone attack on Moscow reported with approximately 10 drones inbound; Russian sources claim 67 drones shot down, Vnukovo and Domodedovo airports closed with mass flight delays. (Source: @eRadarrua/98389)
Ballistic missile threat active with Iskander-M launches possible from Bryansk Oblast and Tsirkon missiles targeting Kyiv. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68114, @mon1tor_ua/68118)

Russian forces struck two gas stations in Zaporozhye with Geranium drones and hit a petroleum product plant near Zaporozhye used in the interest of the AFU. (Source: @tass_agency/382684, @tass_agency/382675)
Ukrainian forces struck the Barrikady plant in Volgograd, damaging production facilities in the Krasnooktyabrsky district, with ten injured reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russia and China conducted joint strategic air patrol over the Sea of East China Sea and western Pacific, with nearly ten aircraft entering South Korea's air defense identification zone, prompting Seoul to scramble fighters. (Source: @UkraineNow/82228, @tass_agency/382682)

Ukraine confirmed 1,878 cases of civilian detention by Russia, but the actual number of civilian hostages may reach 16,000, with Russia attempting to reclassify civilians as prisoners of war to inflate its exchange fund. (Source: @UkraineNow/82224)

Seven Ukrainian civilians aged 35 to 66 were repatriated after being illegally detained by Russia since 2022, from occupied Mariupol, Kyiv, Kharkov, Zaporozhye, and Luhansk regions, including Buchansky district. (Source: @UkraineNow/82226)

GUR MOU issued urgent fundraising appeals for long-range FPV drones on fiber optics to destroy Russian equipment heading to Crimea or the front line, with each drone costing 40,000 UAH. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68125, @mon1tor_ua/68124, @mon1tor_ua/68133)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive overnight drone assault on Ukraine while Ukrainian forces struck the Barrikady plant in Volgograd and conducted drone attacks on Moscow, with Russian authorities claiming 67 drones intercepted and Moscow airports closed. Ballistic missile threats remain acute with Iskander-M launch warnings from Bryansk Oblast and Tsirkon missiles targeting Kyiv. On the ground, Russian forces continue multi-axis offensive operations across Kharkov, Krasny Liman, Konstantinovka, and Zaporozhye sectors while intensifying strikes on Ukrainian locomotives and gas stations with Geranium drones.

points: Ukrainian forces struck the Barrikady plant in Volgograd, damaging production facilities in the Krasnooktyabrsky district, with ten injured reported by the regional governor. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

GUR MOU issued urgent fundraising appeals for long-range FPV drones on fiber optics to destroy Russian equipment heading to Crimea or the front line, with each drone costing 40,000 UAH. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68125, @mon1tor_ua/68133)

Ukrainian channels reported approximately 10 drones heading toward Moscow; Russian sources claim 67 drones shot down, with Vnukovo and Domodedovo airports closed and mass flight delays. (Source: @eRadarrua/98389)

Seven Ukrainian civilians aged 35 to 66 were repatriated after illegal detention by Russia since 2022, from occupied Mariupol, Kyiv, Kharkov, Zaporozhye, and Luhansk regions, including Buchansky district where atrocities occurred. (Source: @UkraineNow/82226)

Ukraine confirmed 1,878 cases of civilian detention by Russia, but the actual number of civilian hostages may reach 16,000; Russia is attempting to reclassify civilians as prisoners of war to inflate its exchange fund. (Source: @UkraineNow/82224)

In Belgorod region, a Ukrainian FPV drone detonated at an enterprise in Shebekino injuring a civilian; a 15-year-old boy was injured in Nikolsky khutor; a man was killed in Ploskoye village, Korochansky district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In the Dnepropetrovsk region, Ukrainian forces captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka–Alexandrovka line; the enemy unsuccessfully attempted to counterattack Trans-Baikal positions with three armored combat vehicles. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian forces struck two gas stations in Zaporozhye with Geranium drones and hit a petroleum product plant (SP Yukoil LLC) near Zaporozhye used in the interest of the AFU. (Source: @tass_agency/382684, @tass_agency/382675)

Russian forces are intensifying strikes against Ukrainian locomotives with remotely controlled Geraniums, destroying 2 under a bridge in Zaporozhye and 3 in the Kharkov region, and targeting gas stations. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

High-intensity strikes including FABs were noted against the city of Zaporozhye; over the previous 24 hours, 18 strikes on settlements were recorded in the Zaporozhye region, killing one and injuring four. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russia and China conducted joint strategic air patrol over the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, and western Pacific, with nearly ten aircraft entering South Korea's air defense identification zone, prompting Seoul to scramble fighters. (Source: @UkraineNow/82228, @tass_agency/382682)
In the Kursk region, Belovsky district, an enemy UAV attacked a grocery store in Belaya settlement wounding a woman; another injured person is in Giri village. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Russian Ministry of Defense recorded 175 drones shot down between 7:00 AM and 8:00 PM; Sevastopol, Crimea, and Sochi also repelled attacks. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Advisor to the Minister of Defense Beskrestnov stated Russia's leadership is ready to wage war in its current format for a very long time, finding 30,000 people monthly to send to the front is not a problem, and Russia will attack with UAVs and missiles for years with China's support. (Source: @UkraineNow/82227)

On the Kharkov direction, Army Group North continues offensive operations in Kazachya Lopan and adjacent forest areas; enemy counterattacks repelled and prisoners taken. On Vovchansk sector, fighting continues in Losevka, Ukrainske, and Zemlyanoy Yar. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Velyky Burluk sector, Russian forces are fighting in Petro-Ivanovka. South of Kupyansk in the Kharkov region, Russian forces are fighting in the area of Kovsharovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
In Krasny Liman, the storming of the city continues; Russian forces are destroying approaching enemy reserves and striking crossings over the Oskol River. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Slavyansk direction, Russian forces continue offensive operations on a broad front, operating from Ray-Aleksandrovka. The storming of Konstantinovka continues with Russian forces building on success in urban areas, shifting FAB strikes to the near rear of the AFU in Druzhkovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In the Dnepropetrovsk region, Army Group East captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka–Alexandrovka line; enemy unsuccessfully attempted to counterattack Trans-Baikal positions with three armored combat vehicles. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In the eastern Zaporozhye region, Far Eastern forces continued wedging into enemy defense west of Vozdvyzhevka, Verkhnya Tersa, and Komsomolske, forcing the enemy to commit reserves on multiple sectors, limiting AFU ability to reinforce Velykomihailovka direction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In the Zaporozhye region, 18 strikes on settlements were recorded over 24 hours with one killed and four injured; AFU continued strikes on Zaporozhye NPP infrastructure. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Sumy direction, in Shostka district, Army Group North assault troops continue small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings. In Sumy district, fighting in Pysarevka and Novaya Sech. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Multiple UAV groups detected heading toward Chernihiv from the north, toward Horodnia in Chernihiv Oblast, and toward Boryspil via Brovary district (Hoholiv, Rusaniv). (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68094, @kpszsu/66580, @eRadarrua/98405)
UAVs detected heading toward Kyiv Oblast north direction toward Chernobyl, and approximately 10 UAVs passed Roishche heading toward Chernihiv. (Source: @kpszsu/66584, @eRadarrua/98387)

In Kharkiv Oblast, reactive UAVs detected in Slobozanskyi district heading toward Balakliia, and UAVs heading toward Vilshans and Nova Vodolaha. A reactive UAV from Belgorod Oblast heading north of Kharkiv Oblast toward Chuhuiv. (Source: @kpszsu/66552, @kpszsu/66550)
In the Kursk region, Belovsky district, an enemy UAV attacked a grocery store in Belaya wounding a woman; another injured person in Giri village. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In Belgorod region, a Ukrainian FPV drone detonated at an enterprise in Shebekino injuring a civilian; a 15-year-old boy injured in Nikolsky khutor; a man killed in Ploskoye village, Korochansky district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In the Kherson region, in Sofiyevka, Kakhovsky district, a boy born in 2010 was injured. In Kakhovka, a man and a woman were wounded. In Dnopryany, a civilian was wounded. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
In Kakhovsky district, an enemy drone deliberately struck an ambulance; a female paramedic was killed and the driver was seriously injured. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In Zaporozhye, three guided aerial bombs damaged a residential high-rise and residential and non-residential buildings; six injured including a 13-year-old girl, per OVA head Ivan Fedorov. (Source: @UkraineNow/82217)

Russian forces struck two gas stations in Zaporozhye with Geranium drones and hit a petroleum product plant (SP Yukoil LLC) near Zaporozhye used in the interest of the AFU. (Source: @tass_agency/382684, @tass_agency/382675)

In the Zaporozhye region, 18 strikes on settlements were recorded over 24 hours with one killed and four injured; AFU continued strikes on Zaporozhye NPP infrastructure. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In the eastern Zaporozhye region, Far Eastern forces continued wedging into enemy defense west of Vozdvyzhevka, Verkhnya Tersa, and Komsomolske, forcing the enemy to commit reserves on multiple sectors. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Enemy tactical aviation active in the Black Sea waters. UAV groups detected heading toward Zaporozhye from the south, and approximately 10 UAVs heading toward Samar/Dnipro from Kharkiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66576, @kpszsu/66556, @eRadarrua/98435)

Massive drone attack on Moscow reported with approximately 10 drones inbound; Russian sources claim 67 drones shot down, Vnukovo and Domodedovo airports closed with mass flight delays. (Source: @eRadarrua/98389)
Ballistic missile threat active with Iskander-M launches possible from Bryansk Oblast and Tsirkon missiles targeting Kyiv. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68114, @mon1tor_ua/68118)
Russian Ministry of Defense recorded 175 drones shot down between 7:00 AM and 8:00 PM; Sevastopol, Crimea, and Sochi also repelled attacks. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Multiple UAV groups detected across northern Ukraine: toward Chernihiv, Horodnia, Boryspil, Chernobyl, Kyiv, Bila Tserkva, Kagarlyk, Vyshhenky, and Obukhiv. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68094, @kpszsu/66580, @eRadarrua/98405, @kpszsu/66584, @mon1tor_ua/68103, @mon1tor_ua/68105, @mon1tor_ua/68101, @mon1tor_ua/68102)

In Kharkiv Oblast, reactive UAVs detected heading toward Balakliia, Chuhuiv, Vilshans, Nova Vodolaha, and Kharkiv from the south; approximately 10 UAVs heading toward Samar/Dnipro from Kharkiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66552, @kpszsu/66550, @kpszsu/66547, @eRadarrua/98435)

Enemy tactical aviation active in the Black Sea waters. UAV groups detected heading toward Zaporozhye from the south and toward Poltava Oblast from Sumy Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66576, @kpszsu/66556, @kpszsu/66559)

Russia and China conducted joint strategic air patrol over the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, and western Pacific, with nearly ten aircraft entering South Korea's air defense identification zone. (Source: @UkraineNow/82228, @tass_agency/382682)

key_developments: Massive drone attack on Moscow with approximately 10 drones inbound; Russian authorities claim 67 drones shot down, Vnukovo and Domodedovo airports closed with mass flight delays. (Source: @eRadarrua/98389)
Ballistic missile threat active with Iskander-M launches possible from Bryansk Oblast and Tsirkon missiles targeting Kyiv. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68114, @mon1tor_ua/68118)
Ukrainian forces struck the Barrikady plant in Volgograd, damaging production facilities in the Krasnooktyabrsky district, with ten injured reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian forces struck two gas stations in Zaporozhye with Geranium drones and hit a petroleum product plant near Zaporozhye used in the interest of the AFU. (Source: @tass_agency/382684, @tass_agency/382675)

Russia and China conducted joint strategic air patrol over the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, and western Pacific, with nearly ten aircraft entering South Korea's air defense identification zone, prompting Seoul to scramble fighters. (Source: @UkraineNow/82228, @tass_agency/382682)

Ukraine confirmed 1,878 cases of civilian detention by Russia, but actual number of civilian hostages may reach 16,000; Russia attempting to reclassify civilians as POWs to inflate exchange fund. (Source: @UkraineNow/82224)

Russian forces continue multi-axis offensives: Kharkov direction (Kazachya Lopan, Vovchansk, Velyky Burluk), Krasny Liman storming, Konstantinovka urban combat, Slavyansk broad front, and Dnepropetrovsk region (Velykomihailovka–Alexandrovka line). (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
sections.threat_assessment: The threat level remains critically high with simultaneous ballistic missile threats (Iskander-M from Bryansk Oblast and Tsirkon targeting Kyiv), massive multi-axis drone attacks across northern, central, and southern Ukraine, and continued Russian ground offensives across the eastern front. The Russian Ministry of Defense recorded 175 drones shot down in a single day, indicating sustained Ukrainian long-range strike operations deep into Russian territory including Moscow, Volgograd, Crimea, and Sochi. The intensification of Russian strikes on Ukrainian locomotives and gas stations with Geranium drones suggests a deliberate effort to degrade Ukrainian logistics and fuel infrastructure. The joint Russia-China air patrol near South Korea demonstrates expanding strategic coordination that could complicate the broader security environment.
sections.regional_response: South Korea scrambled fighters after nearly ten Russian and Chinese aircraft entered its air defense identification zone during a joint patrol over the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, and western Pacific. Finland has permitted the import and transit of nuclear weapons through its territory for national defense purposes but has no plans for permanent deployment. Polish Foreign Minister Sikorski criticized President Nawrocki's reaction to the naming of a Ukrainian Special Forces unit after the Heroes of the Ukrainian Insurgent Army as inadequate, suggesting it made dialogue with Kyiv impossible. These developments indicate shifting regional postures, with NATO members adjusting defense policies and diplomatic tensions emerging between Ukraine and Poland over historical memory issues.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian advisor Beskrestnov's assessment indicates Russia maintains significant capacity for prolonged warfare, with monthly mobilization of 30,000 personnel and sustained UAV/missile production supported by China. The deliberate Russian targeting of locomotives (5 destroyed in Zaporozhye and Kharkov region) and gas stations represents a systematic logistics degradation campaign. The claim that Russia is publicly highlighting fuel difficulties in border regions to mislead Ukrainian targeting suggests an information operation to divert strikes from higher-value targets. The AFU's continued strikes on Zaporozhye NPP infrastructure raise nuclear safety concerns. The large discrepancy between confirmed (1,878) and estimated (16,000) civilian detainees suggests significant intelligence gaps regarding the scale of Russian civilian detention operations.
